bead.title: review: BuildReviewExecuteRequest; remove implHarness default; emit review-pairing-degraded
bead.description:
Today reviewer defaults to implementer harness (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go:521) — directly violating R4 (different reviewer). Build the reviewer ExecuteRequest with: Role='reviewer'; SAME CorrelationID as the implementer call (joins them in events / session log / aggregations); MinPower = implementer.RoutingActual.Power + 1.

'Different provider' Day 1 is BEST-EFFORT EMERGENT — the MinPower bump usually forces Fizeau to pick a different candidate, but not guaranteed. When same-provider results, log a 'review-pairing-degraded' event with implementer + reviewer routing details so operators can see degradation. No ExcludePinned / Exclude-provider field is requested upstream Day 1; future Fizeau routing intelligence may use Role + CorrelationID to bias toward diversity (Fizeau-internal evolution, not specified by DDx).
bead.acceptance: 1. execute_bead_review.go no longer defaults to implementer harness. 2. Reviewer call carries Role='reviewer' + same CorrelationID as implementer + MinPower=actualPower+1. 3. review-pairing-degraded event emitted when reviewer's RoutingActual.Provider == implementer's. 4. Tests cover: happy path (different provider), degraded path (same provider), and event payload includes both routing details.
